intro.title: Introduction
intro.description: AgentsMesh is an AI Agent Fleet Command Center that enables teams to orchestrate AI coding agents like Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, and Aider at scale. Manage remote AI workstations (AgentPod), coordinate multi-agent collaboration (AgentsMesh), and track progress with integrated task management — all from a single platform.

architecture.title: How It Works
architecture.description: AgentsMesh lets you control AI agents running anywhere — on your laptop, a server, or a cloud VM — from your browser. Commands are sent securely over encrypted connections, while terminal output streams in real time. Your code never leaves your infrastructure.
architecture.clientSubtitle: Access from anywhere — browser, phone, or tablet
architecture.controlPlaneDesc: Pod lifecycle, agent commands, status sync
architecture.runnerDesc: Deploy on your own infrastructure — your code never leaves your environment
architecture.agentSubtitle: AI coding agents running in isolated Pods
architecture.securityDesc: Control signals (pod creation, agent commands, status updates) travel through gRPC secured with mutual TLS (mTLS) — each Runner holds a unique X.509 certificate issued by the platform PKI. Terminal data streams (keystrokes, output) flow through the Relay cluster, keeping latency-sensitive I/O on a separate, optimized path. This dual-path design ensures you can securely operate AI agents on any machine, from any device, anywhere in the world.

whatYouCanDo.description: AgentsMesh gives your team superpowers for AI-driven development.

concepts.title: Core Concepts
concepts.description: Understand the foundational concepts behind AgentsMesh to make the most of the platform.

architecture.title: Architecture Overview
architecture.description: AgentsMesh follows a layered architecture where Organizations manage teams, Runners provide compute, and Pods execute AI agents.
architecture.orgDesc: The top-level entity. Each organization has its own members, settings, runners, and billing. Teams can invite members with different roles.
architecture.runnerDesc: A self-hosted daemon that connects to the AgentsMesh backend via gRPC with mTLS. Runners provide the compute environment where Pods execute. You can run multiple Runners across different machines.
architecture.podDesc: An isolated execution environment where an AI coding agent runs. Each Pod has its own PTY terminal, sandbox, and optional Git worktree. Pods are created on Runners and managed through the web interface.
architecture.channelDesc: A communication hub where multiple Pods can exchange messages, share documents, and coordinate work. Channels can be associated with projects and tickets.
architecture.ticketDesc: A task management unit with Kanban board support. Tickets can be linked to Pods for automatic context injection and progress tracking.

byok.title: BYOK (Bring Your Own Key)
byok.description: AgentsMesh uses the BYOK model — you provide your own API keys for AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, etc.). Your API keys are encrypted at rest and never shared. This means you have full control over costs and model selection.

mtls.title: mTLS Security
mtls.description: Runner-to-backend communication is secured with mutual TLS (mTLS). When a Runner registers, it receives a unique X.509 certificate from the platform's PKI. All subsequent gRPC communication is encrypted and mutually authenticated. Certificates are automatically renewed before expiry.

sandbox.title: Sandbox & Git Worktree Isolation
sandbox.description: Each Pod runs in an isolated sandbox environment. When a repository is linked, the Runner creates a dedicated Git worktree so multiple Pods can work on the same repository simultaneously without conflicts. Worktrees are automatically cleaned up when Pods terminate.

mcp.title: MCP (Model Context Protocol)
mcp.description: AgentsMesh provides 24 MCP tools that AI agents can use to discover resources, communicate with other Pods, manage tickets, and control terminals. The MCP server runs on each Runner and is automatically configured for supported agents like Claude Code.

repositoriesGit.title: Repositories & Git Integration
repositoriesGit.description: Connect your Git repositories to AgentsMesh and configure provider access for seamless AI-powered development workflows.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Repositories link your Git projects to the platform. When a Pod is created with a linked repository, the Runner automatically clones the project and sets up an isolated Git worktree. AgentsMesh integrates with popular Git hosting platforms to provide seamless repository access for AI agents.

providers.title: Supported Git Providers
providers.githubDesc: Connect via Personal Access Token (PAT) or OAuth (if you signed up with GitHub). Supports public and private repositories.
providers.gitlabDesc: Connect via Personal Access Token (PAT). Supports self-hosted GitLab instances.
providers.giteeDesc: Connect via Personal Access Token. Popular in the Chinese developer community.

github.title: Connecting GitHub
github.description: There are two ways to connect GitHub, depending on how you signed up:
github.patDesc: Works for all users regardless of sign-up method:
github.step1: In GitHub, go to Settings → Developer settings → Personal access tokens → Fine-grained tokens
github.step2: Click Generate new token and select the repositories you want to grant access to
github.step3: Grant Repository permissions: Contents (Read-only) and Metadata (Read-only) at minimum
github.step4: In AgentsMesh, go to Settings → Personal → Git Settings
github.step5: Click Add GitHub Provider and paste your token
github.oauthTitle: Option B: OAuth (GitHub sign-up users only)
github.oauthDesc: If you registered for AgentsMesh using your GitHub account, OAuth connection is available:
github.oauthStep1: Go to Settings → Personal → Git Settings
github.oauthStep2: Click Connect GitHub — your account is already linked via OAuth
github.oauthStep3: Select which repositories to grant access to
github.oauthNote: OAuth is only available if you signed up with GitHub. Users who registered with email must use a Personal Access Token.

gitlab.title: Connecting GitLab
gitlab.description: Connect GitLab using a Personal Access Token:
gitlab.step1: In GitLab, go to Settings → Access Tokens
gitlab.step2: Create a new token with api and read_repository scopes
gitlab.step3: In AgentsMesh, go to Settings → Personal → Git Settings
gitlab.step4: Click Add GitLab Provider and paste your token
gitlab.step5: Enter your GitLab instance URL (for self-hosted)
gitlab.selfHosted: Self-Hosted GitLab: Enter the full URL of your GitLab instance (e.g., https://gitlab.yourcompany.com).

gitee.title: Connecting Gitee
gitee.description: Connect Gitee using a Personal Access Token:
gitee.step1: In Gitee, go to Settings → Personal Access Tokens
gitee.step2: Create a new token with projects scope
gitee.step3: In AgentsMesh, go to Settings → Personal → Git Settings
gitee.step4: Click Add Gitee Provider and paste your token

sshKeys.title: SSH Key Management
sshKeys.description: For repositories accessed via SSH, ensure your Runner has the correct SSH keys configured:
sshKeys.step1: Generate an SSH key pair on your Runner machine
sshKeys.step2: Add the public key to your Git provider (GitHub/GitLab/Gitee)
sshKeys.step3: The Runner will use the SSH key when cloning repositories
sshKeys.dockerNote: Docker Runners: Mount your SSH keys as a read-only volume: -v ~/.ssh:/root/.ssh:ro

podUsage.title: Using Repositories in Pods
podUsage.description: Once a repository is connected, you can link it to Pods:
podUsage.step1: When creating a new Pod, select a repository from the dropdown
podUsage.step2: The Runner clones the repo and creates an isolated Git worktree
podUsage.step3: The AI agent starts in the repository's working directory
podUsage.step4: All changes are isolated to the Pod's worktree

worktree.title: Git Worktree Isolation
worktree.description: Git worktrees allow multiple Pods to work on the same repository simultaneously. Each Pod operates on its own branch without affecting others. This is critical for parallel AI agent development where multiple agents may modify the same codebase.

management.title: Managing Repositories
management.addRepoSteps: Go to Settings → Repositories → Add Repository. Select your Git provider, authenticate, and choose the repositories to connect.
management.removeRepoSteps: Removing a repository disconnects it from AgentsMesh. Existing Pods using this repository will not be affected, but new Pods cannot be created with it.

agentfile.title: AgentFile Syntax Reference
agentfile.description: Complete reference for AgentFile — the domain-specific language (DSL) that defines how AI agent execution environments are configured in AgentsMesh.

whatIs.title: What is AgentFile?
whatIs.description: AgentFile is a declarative DSL for configuring AI agent execution environments, similar to how Dockerfile defines container builds. Each agent in AgentsMesh has an AgentFile that specifies the CLI command, environment variables, configuration options, and build logic needed to launch the agent.

structure.title: Structure
structure.description: An AgentFile consists of two types of statements: declarations and build logic.
structure.declarationsDesc: Top-level keywords that define the agent's identity, configuration schema, environment, and integrations. Examples: AGENT, CONFIG, ENV, MODE, MCP.
structure.buildLogicDesc: Imperative statements that construct CLI arguments, write files, and apply conditional logic at build time. Examples: arg, file, mkdir, if/else, for/in.

declarations.title: Declaration Keywords
declarations.description: Declarations define the agent's configuration schema and runtime environment. They are written in UPPERCASE.
declarations.agentDesc: The CLI command used to launch the agent. Accepts a quoted string with the command and optional default arguments.
declarations.executableDesc: The name of the executable binary. Used by the Runner to verify the agent is installed.
declarations.configDesc: Defines a user-configurable option. Types: BOOL, STRING, NUMBER, SECRET, SELECT("opt1","opt2"). Optionally set a default value with = .
declarations.envDesc: Declares an environment variable. Use SECRET for sensitive values, TEXT for plain text, OPTIONAL to mark it non-required. Use = "value" for static values.
declarations.modeDesc: Sets the execution mode. 'pty' runs the agent in a PTY terminal. 'acp' uses the Agent Communication Protocol with optional arguments.
declarations.credentialDesc: Specifies which credential profile to use. Use a quoted profile name or 'runner_host' to use the Runner machine's credentials.
declarations.promptDesc: Sets the initial prompt sent to the agent. PROMPT_POSITION controls where the prompt is injected: prepend (before user prompt), append (after), or none (disabled).
declarations.repoDesc: REPO sets the repository by slug or URL. BRANCH sets the branch name. GIT_CREDENTIAL selects the Git authentication method: http, ssh, or token.
declarations.mcpDesc: Enables or disables MCP tool integration. When ON, an optional FORMAT can specify the protocol format (e.g., streamable-http).
declarations.skillsDesc: Attaches reusable skill definitions to the agent. Skills are referenced by their slug, separated by commas.
declarations.setupDesc: A shell script that runs before the agent starts. Use heredoc syntax (<<LABEL...LABEL). Optional timeout= parameter sets the maximum execution time in seconds.
declarations.removeDesc: Removes a previously defined item. Can remove ENV variables, SKILLS, CLI arguments (arg), or files (file).

buildLogic.title: Build Logic
buildLogic.description: Build logic statements are lowercase and execute during the AgentFile evaluation phase. They construct CLI arguments, write files, and apply conditional logic.

expressions.title: Expressions
expressions.description: AgentFile supports a variety of expression types that can be used in build logic and declarations.

builtinVars.title: Built-in Variables
builtinVars.description: These variables are automatically available during AgentFile evaluation and provide access to configuration values and runtime context.
builtinVars.configDesc: User-provided configuration values defined by CONFIG declarations
builtinVars.sandboxRoot: Root directory of the sandbox environment
builtinVars.sandboxWorkDir: Working directory inside the sandbox (may differ from root when a repo is cloned)
builtinVars.mcpEnabled: Boolean indicating whether MCP is enabled
builtinVars.mcpServers: List of MCP server configuration paths

agentfileLayer.title: AgentFile Layer
agentfileLayer.description: AgentFile Layers let you override agent configuration on a per-Pod basis without modifying the base AgentFile.

whatIs.title: What is AgentFile Layer?
whatIs.description: An AgentFile Layer is a user-provided override that is merged on top of the base AgentFile at Pod creation time. AgentsMesh uses a 3-tier merge model: the base AgentFile (agent defaults) is merged with user preferences (saved settings), then the Layer (per-Pod runtime overrides) is applied last. The Layer takes highest priority.

gettingStarted.title: Quick Start
gettingStarted.description: Get AgentsMesh up and running in six steps. This is a quick overview — each step links to a detailed tutorial with troubleshooting and advanced options.

step1.title: Create an Account
step1.description: Register at the sign-up page with your email, username, and password. After submitting you will receive a verification email — check your spam folder if it does not arrive within a few minutes.
step1.whatYouGet: After verifying your email, the onboarding wizard will guide you to:
step1.item1: Create a personal workspace (one click) or a team workspace
step1.item2: Optionally join an existing organization via an invitation link

step2.title: Setup a Runner
step2.description: A Runner is a lightweight daemon that runs on your machine and executes AI agents in isolated workspaces called Pods. Install the AgentsMesh Runner on your development machine or server.
step2.seeSetup: See {link} for Docker, Linux packages, and Kubernetes deployment options.

step3.title: Install AI Agent CLIs
step3.description: Install the CLI tools you want to use on your Runner machine and set the required API key environment variables. The Runner auto-detects installed CLIs via PATH and reports available agents to the platform.
step3.tip: After installing, restart the Runner or wait for the next heartbeat. The newly available agents will appear in the New Pod dialog.
step3.seeSetup: See {link} for more details on MCP tools and skill configuration.

step4.title: Connect a Git Provider
step4.description: Connecting a Git provider lets your Pods clone repositories and work in isolated Git worktrees. Go to Settings → Personal → Git Settings to add a provider.
step4.githubDesc: Add a Personal Access Token with the repo scope.
step4.gitlabDesc: Add a Personal Access Token with api and read_repository scopes. Self-hosted instances are supported.
step4.seeGuide: See {link} for full setup instructions including SSH keys and Gitee.

step5.title: Start a Pod
step5.description: A Pod (AgentPod) is a virtual workstation where an AI agent runs with its own terminal and isolated code workspace. Create your first one now!
step5.item1: Navigate to Workspace in the sidebar
step5.item3: Select an AI agent (Claude Code, Codex CLI, etc.)
step5.item5: Optionally select a repository and branch — the agent will work in an isolated copy (Git worktree) so your main branch stays safe
step5.item6: Optionally link to a ticket for context
step5.item7: Add an initial prompt describing the task (e.g., "Add input validation to the login form" or "Fix the failing test in auth.test.ts")
step5.seeSetup: See {link} for a detailed walkthrough with troubleshooting.

step6.title: Interact with Your Agent
step6.description: After creation the terminal view opens with a live session. Your AI agent begins executing the initial prompt or waits for input if none was provided.
step6.item1: Type follow-up prompts or commands directly in the terminal
step6.item2: Watch the agent status indicator — Idle (ready for input), Executing (working on your task), or Waiting (needs your approval)
step6.item3: The agent works inside the repository worktree if one was linked
step6.item4: Terminate the Pod from the toolbar when your task is complete

tryIt.title: Try It: Your First Pod in 60 Seconds
tryIt.description: Want to see it work right away? Here's the fastest path — no repository needed:
tryIt.item2: Select Claude Code (or any installed agent) and your Runner
tryIt.item3: Skip the repository and ticket fields
tryIt.item4: Set the initial prompt to: "Say hello, then list the files in the current directory and explain what you see."
tryIt.item5: Click Create and watch the agent respond in the terminal
tryIt.tip: This creates a Pod without a repository — the agent runs in a temporary directory. It's perfect for testing your setup before working with real code.

agentpod.title: AgentPod
agentpod.description: AgentPod provides remote AI development workstations with integrated terminal access. Each Pod runs on a Runner and provides a full development environment with AI assistance.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: An AgentPod is an isolated development environment where an AI coding agent (like Claude Code) runs with full terminal access. Each Pod includes:
overview.terminal: Web Terminal — Real-time terminal access via WebSocket
overview.worktree: Git Worktree — Isolated workspace per Pod
overview.agent: AI Agent — Your chosen coding assistant
overview.mcpTools: MCP Tools — 24 tools for discovery, terminal control, collaboration, and more

keyFeatures.title: Key Features
keyFeatures.webTerminalDesc: Full terminal emulation in your browser with WebSocket support for real-time interaction. No local setup required.
keyFeatures.statusMonitoringDesc: Real-time visibility into what your AI agent is doing: idle, working, waiting for input, or finished.
keyFeatures.worktreeIsolationDesc: Each Pod gets its own Git worktree, ensuring complete isolation between concurrent Pods.
keyFeatures.multiplePodsDesc: Run multiple AI agent Pods concurrently, each working on different tasks or branches.
keyFeatures.ticketIntegrationDesc: Link Pods to tickets for automatic context and progress tracking.
keyFeatures.autoCleanupDesc: Pods are automatically cleaned up when terminated, freeing resources.

lifecycle.title: Pod Lifecycle
lifecycle.initializingDesc: Pod is being created, worktree is being set up, and agent is starting.
lifecycle.runningDesc: Pod is active and ready for interaction.
lifecycle.pausedDesc: Pod is temporarily paused (agent not active).
lifecycle.disconnectedDesc: Runner lost connection but Pod may recover.
lifecycle.orphanedDesc: Runner is restarting. Session will resume automatically.
lifecycle.errorDesc: Pod encountered an error during execution.

agentStatus.title: Agent Status
agentStatus.description: Within a running Pod, the AI agent has its own status:
agentStatus.idleDesc: Agent is waiting for a task or prompt
agentStatus.executingDesc: Agent is actively executing a task
agentStatus.waitingDesc: Agent is waiting for user input or approval

configuration.title: Pod Configuration
configuration.description: When creating a Pod, you can configure:
configuration.agentDesc: Claude Code, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Aider, or custom
configuration.permissionModeDesc: bypassPermissions (auto-approve all, default), default (per-tool approval), acceptEdits (auto-approve file edits), dontAsk (deny unless allowlisted), or plan (read-only planning)
configuration.repositoryDesc: Link to a Git repository for automatic cloning
configuration.ticketDesc: Link to a ticket for context and tracking
configuration.promptDesc: Starting instructions for the AI agent

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.description: The Workspace is your central hub for managing and interacting with all active AgentPods.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The Workspace provides a multi-panel view where you can monitor and interact with multiple Pods simultaneously. It's designed for developers who run several AI agents in parallel.

multiTerminal.title: Multi-Terminal View
multiTerminal.description: View multiple terminal sessions side by side. Each terminal connects to a different Pod, allowing you to monitor AI agent activity across your projects.
multiTerminal.splitViewDesc: Arrange terminals in horizontal or vertical splits to compare output across Pods.
multiTerminal.fullscreenDesc: Focus on a single terminal in fullscreen for detailed interaction.
multiTerminal.tabbingDesc: Quickly switch between Pods using keyboard shortcuts or tab clicks.

podList.title: Pod Management
podList.description: The pod list sidebar shows all your Pods with real-time status indicators. Filter by status, agent, or repository to quickly find what you need.
podList.statusFiltersDesc: Filter Pods by Running, Paused, Completed, or Error states.
podList.quickActionsDesc: Terminate, pause, or restart Pods directly from the list.
podList.detailsDesc: Click any Pod to view its configuration, linked ticket, and resource usage.

quickCreate.title: Quick Create
quickCreate.description: Launch new Pods quickly from the Workspace with the Quick Create dialog. Select an agent, choose a runner, and optionally link a repository — all in a few clicks.
quickCreate.steps: Steps: Choose Agent → Select Runner → (Optional) Link Repository → Create

realTime.title: Real-Time Interaction
realTime.description: All terminal interactions are streamed in real-time via WebSocket. Type commands, provide input to AI agents, and see results instantly — just like a local terminal.

channels.title: Channels
channels.description: Channels provide communication hubs for AI agents. Multiple Pods can join a channel to collaborate on tasks, share information, and coordinate work.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Channels are persistent communication spaces where AI agents can:

creating.title: Creating Channels
creating.description: Channels can be created with optional associations:
creating.name: Name
creating.channelDescriptionDesc: Optional description of the channel's purpose
creating.ticketSlugDesc: Link to a specific ticket (e.g., AM-123)

messageTypes.title: Message Types
messageTypes.textDesc: Plain text messages for general communication between agents. This is the default message type.
messageTypes.systemDesc: System notifications like Pod joins, leaves, and status updates. Generated automatically by the platform.

mentions.title: Mentions
mentions.description: Messages can mention specific Pods to get their attention:
mentions.hint: Mentioned Pods can filter messages using the {param} parameter when fetching messages.

sharedDocs.title: Shared Documents
sharedDocs.description: Each channel has a shared document that agents can collaboratively edit:
sharedDocs.getDocDesc: Retrieve the current shared document content.
sharedDocs.updateDocDesc: Update the shared document with new content.
sharedDocs.hint: Use shared documents for collaborative notes, specifications, or any content that needs to persist across messages.

mcpTools.title: Channel MCP Tools
mcpTools.searchChannelsDesc: Search for channels by name, project, or ticket
mcpTools.sendMessageDesc: Send a message to a channel

useCases.title: Use Cases
useCases.coordinationDesc: Create a channel for a ticket and have multiple agents discuss implementation approach, share findings, and coordinate work.
useCases.designDocsDesc: Use the shared document feature to collaboratively write technical specifications or architecture decisions.
useCases.notificationsDesc: Broadcast important updates to all agents working on a project using system messages.

webUI.title: Using Channels in the Web UI
webUI.description: You can also manage channels directly from the web interface:
webUI.step1: Navigate to the Mesh view from the sidebar
webUI.step2: Open the channel panel on the right side
webUI.step3: Create new channels or select existing ones
webUI.step4: View message history and shared documents
webUI.step5: See which Pods are participating in real-time

mesh.title: Mesh Topology
mesh.description: Visualize and manage the collaboration network between your AI agent Pods in real-time.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: The Mesh view provides an interactive topology visualization showing how your Pods are connected and collaborating. It's the central control panel for multi-agent workflows.

visualization.title: Topology Visualization
visualization.description: The mesh graph renders Pods as nodes and Bindings as edges. The layout updates in real-time as Pods connect and disconnect.
visualization.edges: Edges represent active Bindings between Pods
visualization.animation: Animated edges show active data flow

binding.title: Pod Binding Mechanism
binding.description: Bindings establish permission-controlled connections between Pods. A binding allows one Pod to interact with another's terminal or resources.
binding.podRead: pod:read — Observe another Pod's terminal output
binding.podWrite: pod:write — Send input to another Pod's terminal
binding.flowStep1: Pod A requests a binding to Pod B with specific scopes
binding.flowStep2: Pod B receives the binding request
binding.flowStep3: Pod B accepts or rejects the binding
binding.flowStep4: If accepted, Pod A can use the granted scopes

channelIntegration.title: Channel Integration
channelIntegration.description: The Mesh view includes a channel panel on the right side where you can view and participate in channel conversations. Select a channel to see messages and shared documents alongside the topology view.

monitoring.title: Real-Time Monitoring
monitoring.description: The Mesh view updates in real-time via WebSocket connections. You can observe:

tickets.title: Tickets
tickets.description: Integrated task management with Kanban board view. Create tickets, assign them to AgentPods, and track progress through your workflow.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: AgentsMesh Tickets provide a lightweight issue tracking system designed for AI-driven development. Key features include:
overview.item3: Git commit and merge request associations

status.title: Ticket Status
status.description: Tickets flow through these statuses in the Kanban board:

priority.title: Priority Levels
priority.highDesc: Important tasks that should be addressed soon

podIntegration.title: Pod Integration
podIntegration.context: Provide context — The AI agent receives ticket details as initial context
podIntegration.progress: Track progress — See which Pods are working on which tickets
podIntegration.autoUpdate: Auto-update — Pod completion can update ticket status
podIntegration.history: View history — See all Pods that worked on a ticket

gitIntegration.title: Git Integration
gitIntegration.commitsDesc: Link commits to tickets by SHA. View commit messages, authors, and links to the source.
gitIntegration.mrsDesc: Track MRs/PRs associated with tickets. See status, pipeline results, and review state.

storyPoints.title: Story Points
storyPoints.description: Estimate effort using Fibonacci story points:

loops.title: Loops
loops.description: CI/CD for AI Agent tasks. Define repeatable automated tasks and trigger them on a schedule or via API — like cron jobs for your AI agents.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Loops let you define a task once and run it repeatedly. Each Loop wraps a Pod with a prompt template, trigger configuration, and execution policy. Key features include:
overview.item3: Persistent sandbox reuse across runs for incremental work
overview.item5: Webhook callbacks for run completion notifications

executionModes.title: Execution Modes
executionModes.autopilotDesc: A Control Agent supervises the execution. It monitors progress, handles errors, and decides when the task is complete. Best for complex, multi-step workflows.
executionModes.directDesc: The agent runs the prompt autonomously. The Pod terminates automatically when the agent finishes. Best for simple, well-defined tasks.

triggerTypes.title: Trigger Types
triggerTypes.cronDesc: Run on a recurring schedule using standard cron expressions (e.g., '0 9 * * *' for daily at 9 AM UTC). The scheduler checks every 30 seconds for due Loops.
triggerTypes.apiDesc: Trigger runs programmatically via HTTP POST. Pass runtime variables to customize each run. Ideal for CI/CD pipelines, webhooks, and event-driven automation.

sandboxStrategies.title: Sandbox Strategies
sandboxStrategies.description: Control how the execution environment is managed across runs:
sandboxStrategies.persistentDesc: Reuse the same sandbox across runs. File changes, git state, and installed packages carry over. Uses Pod Resume to restore the previous environment.
sandboxStrategies.freshDesc: Create a new sandbox for each run. Guarantees a clean starting state every time. Best for tasks that need reproducible environments.

sessionPersistence.title: Session Persistence
sessionPersistence.description: When enabled, the agent retains conversation history across runs. The same SessionID is reused, so the agent remembers context from previous executions. Disable this for independent runs without historical context.

concurrency.title: Concurrency Policies
concurrency.description: Control what happens when a new run is triggered while another is still active:
concurrency.skipDesc: The new run is skipped if the maximum concurrent runs is reached.
concurrency.queueDesc: The new run is queued and starts when a slot becomes available.
concurrency.replaceDesc: The active run is cancelled and replaced by the new one.

promptTemplates.title: Prompt Templates
promptTemplates.description: Use {{variable}} placeholders in your prompt. Define default values when creating the Loop, and override them at trigger time. Example:
promptTemplates.example: Review all changed files in {{branch}} branch since last review. Focus on {{focus_area}} issues.

webhookCallbacks.title: Webhook Callbacks
webhookCallbacks.description: Configure a callback URL to receive HTTP POST notifications when a run completes. The payload includes run status, duration, exit summary, and error details. Use this to integrate with Slack, PagerDuty, or custom monitoring.

useCases.item2: Nightly dependency updates and security scanning

repositories.description: Connect your Git repositories to AgentsMesh for seamless AI-powered development workflows.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Repositories link your Git projects to the platform. When a Pod is created with a linked repository, the Runner automatically clones the project and sets up an isolated Git worktree.

providers.title: Supported Git Providers
providers.githubDesc: Connect via OAuth for seamless authorization. Supports public and private repositories.
providers.gitlabDesc: Connect via Personal Access Token (PAT). Supports self-hosted GitLab instances.
